jsonLdWeb.name: Concrete Bag Calculator
jsonLdWeb.description: Calculate bags of concrete needed for any project with cost and pallet estimates.

mainEntity.name: How many 80 lb bags of concrete are in a cubic yard?

acceptedAnswer.text: One 80 lb bag yields approximately 0.60 cubic feet. One cubic yard equals 27 cubic feet. So you need 27 ÷ 0.60 = 45 bags of 80 lb concrete per cubic yard.

mainEntity.name: How many bags of concrete do I need for a 10×10 slab?

acceptedAnswer.text: A 10×10 ft slab at 4 inches thick requires 10 × 10 × 0.333 = 33.3 cubic feet. With 80 lb bags at 0.60 cu ft each, you need 33.3 ÷ 0.60 = 56 bags. With 60 lb bags, you need 74 bags.

mainEntity.name: What is the coverage per bag of concrete?

acceptedAnswer.text: A 40 lb bag covers 0.30 cubic feet, a 60 lb bag covers 0.45 cubic feet, and an 80 lb bag covers 0.60 cubic feet. These are the standard yields for premixed concrete like Quikrete or Sakrete.

mainEntity.name: How many bags fit on a pallet?

acceptedAnswer.text: A standard pallet holds about 42 bags of 80 lb concrete (3,360 lbs), 56 bags of 60 lb concrete (3,360 lbs), or 80 bags of 40 lb concrete (3,200 lbs). Pallet counts vary slightly by manufacturer.

mainEntity.name: Should I use 60 lb or 80 lb bags?

acceptedAnswer.text: 80 lb bags are more cost-effective per cubic foot but harder to handle. 60 lb bags are easier to carry and mix by hand. For large projects, 80 lb bags save money and trips. For small repairs or solo work, 60 lb bags are more manageable.

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate concrete volume for a project?

acceptedAnswer.text: Multiply length × width × depth (all in feet) to get cubic feet. Divide by 27 to convert to cubic yards. For a 10 ft × 12 ft × 4 in slab: 10 × 12 × 0.333 = 40 cu ft = 1.48 cu yd.

howItWorks:
How to Calculate Concrete Bags

First determine the total volume of concrete needed, then divide by the yield per bag based on bag size.

Bags = Volume (cu ft) ÷ Yield per Bag (cu ft)
  • Volume from dimensions: Length × Width × Depth (convert depth to feet)
  • 40 lb bag: 0.30 cu ft yield → 90 bags/cu yd
  • 60 lb bag: 0.45 cu ft yield → 60 bags/cu yd
  • 80 lb bag: 0.60 cu ft yield → 45 bags/cu yd
  • Pallet: ~42 bags of 80 lb, ~56 bags of 60 lb, ~80 bags of 40 lb
  • Add 5–10% extra for waste and spillage
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

How many 80 lb bags per cubic yard?

45 bags. One cubic yard = 27 cu ft, and each 80 lb bag yields 0.60 cu ft. 27 ÷ 0.60 = 45 bags.

How many bags for a 10×10 slab?

At 4 inches thick: 10 × 10 × 0.333 = 33.3 cu ft. With 80 lb bags: 56 bags. With 60 lb bags: 74 bags.

What's the coverage per bag?

40 lb = 0.30 cu ft, 60 lb = 0.45 cu ft, 80 lb = 0.60 cu ft. These are standard yields for Quikrete and Sakrete premixed concrete.

How many bags fit on a pallet?

About 42 bags of 80 lb, 56 bags of 60 lb, or 80 bags of 40 lb per standard pallet.

Should I use 60 lb or 80 lb bags?

80 lb bags are more cost-effective but harder to handle. 60 lb bags are better for hand mixing and solo work. For large jobs, 80 lb bags save money.

How do I calculate volume for my project?

Length × Width × Depth in feet = cubic feet. Divide by 27 for cubic yards. Remember to convert inches to feet (divide by 12).
